role_summary: Mississippi civil rights movement participant; one half of the Hamilton/Ulmer joint oral history (catalog entry 4).
ai_reading: Audrey Nell Hamilton shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 4 (catalog slug 'audrey-nell-hamilton-and-joeann-anderson-ulmer'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Audrey Nell Hamilton is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 4 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Audrey Nell Hamilton; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Audrey Nell Hamilton is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 4
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: St. Augustine Foot Soldiers Monument, Plaza de la Constitucion, St. Augustine, Florida
gallery.caption: The St. Augustine Foot Soldiers Monument honors the people who marched and sat in during the 1960s St. Augustine movement, the setting of Hamilton's oral history.
